title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Life Insurance - Smoker Vs non-Smokers
Did you know that smoking is not just hazardous to health but to your term insurance plan as well? While a smoker can avail of term insurance, the premiums would be much higher than that of premiums for a similar plan availed by a non-smoker. Here is how a term insurance policy’s terms and conditions would differ between smokers and non-smokers.
Who is a smoker, according to life insurance companies?
Any individual who consumes nicotine or tobacco is considered a smoker as per insurers. The consumption of tobacco or nicotine can be in the form of gutka, pan masala, bidi, cigarettes etc. Your habits regarding smoking should be informed and declared in the insurance application. There are a set of questions that insurer uses to assess your smoking habits. Some of the questions are:
Do you smoke?
How frequently do you smoke?
What nicotine/tobacco products have you consumed?
When was the last time you smoked?
How many cigarettes/other tobacco products do you consume in a day?
Categorisation of smokers and non-smokers:
Apart from the broad classification of smokers and non-smokers, smokers are further categorised: into the below-mentioned types:
Preferred Smoker: Applicant who smokes but is fit and healthy
Typical Smoker: The applicant smokes and has minor health issues
Table – rate smoker: Applicant has severe health issues due to his smoking habits.
Term insurance plans for smokers and non-smokers:
Both smokers and non-smokers are eligible to avail of term insurance plans. However, depending on which category they fall into, the risk factors differ. Based on the risk factors, the premium price is determined.
Why is the premium higher for term insurance plans for smokers?
The premiums for an applicant who is a smoker would be higher when compared to the same plan for a non-smoker. The risks associated with smoking include health hazards like chronic diseases, lung cancer, heart ailments, respiratory diseases, bronchitis etc. Smokers also have a lower life expectancy ratio, and the probability of claim within the said tenure is higher. These are some of the reasons why a smoker’s term insurance plan will cost higher than that of a non-smoker’s term insurance plan.
What happens if your declarations about smoking in term insurance plans are inaccurate?
Many smokers do not disclose their smoking habits honestly. This could affect their claims at a later stage. The intent behind not disclosing smoking habits is to avoid payment of higher premiums. This could be counterproductive as they may lose out on genuine claims at a later stage. Most such lies get detected as there will be traces of nicotine in urine samples during the medical examination before issuance of a term insurance policy. In case, such falsehood is detected at any point. The insurer will have the option to terminate the policy and charge the policyholder with a fraud case.
Choice of term insurance plans for Smokers vs non-smokers:
Here are some stark differences between term insurance plans for smokers and non-smokers.
The premium has been calculated by using a Term Insurance calculator1 for a policy upto 60 years of age. However, the exact premium would depend on your age, income details, pre-existing ailments, etc. The term insurance benefits remain the same once the policy has been issued as the risk of the insured’s life gets transferred from the insured to the insurer in lieu of premium payment.
Irrespective of whether you are a smoker or non-smoker, you need to consider term insurance plans which have wider coverage at optimal costs. The claims process and settlement ratio should also be considered. A plan which offers coverage for the longest time period should be availed. Availing of a term insurance plan will provide financial security to your family in the event of your untimely demise.
Irrespective of whether you are a smoker or non-smoker, you need to always need insurance to hedge your life risk. Honest and accurate declarations are critical to seamless issuance of policy and timely payouts upon claims. Any information that you do not disclose to your insurer will lead to problems in claims management. The risks associated with a smoker are higher, and hence, the premiums would be correspondingly high. Choose to associate with the right plan which aligns well with your requirements.
